content.cn_text: 现在你将听到一个名为“慈善-水”的慈善机构的水项目经理的演讲。
content.en_text: You will now listen to a talk by the Water Project Manager of a charity called 'Charity-Water'.

content.cn_text: 在播放录音之前，你有些时间阅读问题11至问题17。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 11 to 17.

content.cn_text: (暂停录音30秒)
content.en_text: (Pause the recording for 30 seconds)

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答问题11至问题17。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 17.

content.cn_text: 作为慈善水组织的水项目经理，我前往地球上一些最绝望的地方寻找干净的水。
content.en_text: As Charity-Water's Water Project Manager, I travel to some of the most desperate places on earth in search of clean water.

content.cn_text: 尽管风景在变化，但有一件事是不变的:女人总是在走路。
content.en_text: And while the landscape changes, there's always one thing that remains the same: the women are always walking.

content.cn_text: 无论我是在海地的山区，利比里亚的农村，还是中非共和国的丛林，妇女们总是在挑水。
content.en_text: Whether I'm in the mountains of Haiti, in rural Liberia, or the jungles of the Central African Republic, the women are always carrying water.

content.cn_text: 问12:让你了解一下慈善水组织所做的工作。
content.en_text: To give you an idea of the work that Charity-Water does.

content.cn_text: 我来告诉你其中一个女人的故事。
content.en_text: I'll tell you the story of one of these women.

content.cn_text: 我们的卡车行驶在乌干达北部中部一条崎岖不平的道路上，突然转向驶离道路，翻上了路堤。
content.en_text: Driving down a bumpy road in the middle of Northern Uganda, our truck suddenly swerves off the road and up over an embankment.

content.cn_text: 问13:我们通常喜欢在到达时给社区一个惊喜，因为这样在没有数百人围观的情况下，更容易监测供水点的运行情况。
content.en_text: We usually prefer to surprise communities by our arrival because it makes it easier to monitor how our water points are functioning without hundreds of people watching.

content.cn_text: 但一旦你拜访了附近的几个社区，关于你存在的谣言就会像野火一样蔓延开来。
content.en_text: But once you visit a few communities in the neighbourhood, rumours of your presence spread like wildfire.

content.cn_text: 我们跳出卡车，走进一个派对。
content.en_text: We jump out of the truck and walk into a party.

content.cn_text: 这时我遇到了海伦·阿皮斯。
content.en_text: This is when I met Helen Apis.

content.cn_text: 她给我讲了她村里新开的一口淡水井。
content.en_text: She told me about the new freshwater well in her village.

content.cn_text: “我现在很开心，”海伦笑道。
content.en_text: "I am happy now," Helen beamed.

content.cn_text: “我有时间吃饭，我的孩子可以去上学。我甚至可以在我的花园里工作，洗个澡，然后如果我想的话，再回来取水!
content.en_text: "I have time to eat, my children can go to school.And I can even work in my garden, take a shower and then come back for more water if I want!

content.cn_text: 我洗澡洗得很好。”
content.en_text: I am bathing so well."

content.cn_text: 听到一个女人谈论洗澡，几个男人咯咯地笑了起来。
content.en_text: A few of the men chuckled to hear a woman talk about bathing.

content.cn_text: 但我所注意到的是海伦容光焕发的脸，她头发上的鲜花，以及她在特殊场合穿的那件可爱的绿色连衣裙。
content.en_text: But all I noticed was Helen's glowing face, the fresh flowers in her hair, and the lovely green dress she wore for special occasions.

content.cn_text: 我摸了摸她的前臂，回答说:“嗯，你看起来很棒。”
content.en_text: Touching her forearm, I replied, 'Well, you look great."

content.cn_text: “是的,”她停顿了一下。
content.en_text: "Yes,"she paused.

content.cn_text: 她把双手放在我的肩膀上，微笑着说:“现在，我很漂亮了。”
content.en_text: Placing both hands on my shoulders and smiling, she said, "Now, I am beautiful."

content.cn_text: 这真的打击了我。
content.en_text: That really hit me.

content.cn_text: 我的工作重点是可持续发展、健康、个人卫生和环境卫生;以确保慈善水项目在20年内继续运作。但在我的所有调查或评估中，都找不到写作的地方。
content.en_text: My job is to focus on sustainable development, health,hygiene and sanitation; to make sure Charity-Water's projects are working in 20 years. But nowhere on any of my surveys or evaluations was a place to write.

content.cn_text: “今天我们让某人觉得自己很美。”
content.en_text: "Today we made someone feel beautiful."

content.cn_text: 在听剩下的内容之前，你有些时间看看第十八题至第二十题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you will have some time to look at questions 18 to 20.

content.cn_text: (暂停录音30秒)
content.en_text: (Pause the recording for 30 seconds)

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答第十八题至第二十题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 18 to 20.

content.cn_text: 在没有干净的水之前，海伦会在黎明前起床，只带着两个5加仑的杰里罐，步行近一英里半到最近的供水点，而这个供水点恰好在一所学校里。
content.en_text: Before she had clean water, Helen would wake up before dawn, take her only two 5-gallon Jerry Cans, and walk almost a mile and a half to the nearest water point, which happens to be at a school.

content.cn_text: 因为没有足够的水供该地区的人口使用，她和其他数百名同样重视清洁水的妇女一起排队等候。海伦唯一的其他选择就是跳过等待，从池塘里收集受污染的水。
content.en_text: Because there simply wasn't enough water for the area's population, she'd wait in line with hundreds of other women who also valued clean water.Helen's only other option was to skip the wait and collect contaminated water from a pond.

content.cn_text: 海伦一天中大部分时间都在散步。
content.en_text: Helen spent most of her day walking and.

content.cn_text: 等待。
content.en_text: waiting.

content.cn_text: 她告诉我，她每天都会对自己说:“我今天应该怎么用这杯水?”
content.en_text: She told me each day she'd say to herself, "How should I use this water today?

content.cn_text: 我应该给我的花园浇水，这样我们就可以种食物了吗?
content.en_text: Should I water my garden so we can grow food?

content.cn_text: 我应该洗孩子们的校服吗?
content.en_text: Should I wash my children's uniforms?

content.cn_text: 我应该用它做饭吗?
content.en_text: Should I use it to cook a meal?

content.cn_text: 我们应该喝这水吗?”
content.en_text: Should we drink this water?"

content.cn_text: 海伦有两个孩子，一个丈夫和10加仑汽油，她必须做出选择。
content.en_text: With two children, one husband and 10 gallons, Helen had to make choices.

content.cn_text: 当她描述她如何从长途跋涉回来，发现她的两个年幼的孩子在等她时，我看到了她眼中的羞愧。
content.en_text: I saw the shame in her eyes when she described how she would return from her long trek to find her two young children waiting for her.

content.cn_text: 他们经常因为校服脏而被学校打发回家。
content.en_text: They were often sent home from school because their uniforms were dirty.

content.cn_text: 村子里有了新井，她的生活发生了变化。
content.en_text: With the new well in her village, her life was transformed.

content.cn_text: 她现在有了选择:空闲时间;选项。
content.en_text: She now had choices: free time; options.

content.cn_text: 同时，海伦被选为水
content.en_text: Also, Helen had been chosen to be the Water

content.cn_text: 委员会司库，向51户住户收取名义上的费用，用于维修水井。
content.en_text: Committee Treasurer, collecting nominal fees from 51 households to use for the maintenance of their well.

content.cn_text: 水委员会往往是妇女第一次被选为村庄的领导职位。
content.en_text: Water Committees are often the first time women ever get elected to leadership positions in villages.

content.cn_text: 上个月，海伦在排队等水。
content.en_text: Last month, Helen was standing in line waiting for water.

content.cn_text: 这个月，她为她的社区挺身而出。
content.en_text: This month, she's standing up for her community.

content.cn_text: 现在，她很漂亮。
content.en_text: And now, she is beautiful.

content.cn_text: 第二部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part two.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: (暂停录音30秒)
content.en_text: (Pause the recording for 30 seconds)

content.cn_text: 现在转到第三部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to Part three.

groups.desc:
Questions 11-17
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: The speaker's job requires
items.analysis: 题目询问说话人的工作需要什么。从答案句中可以看出，作为水务项目经理，说话人需要前往地球上最绝望的地方寻找清洁水源。这明显表明他的工作需要大量的旅行，因此B选项"extensive travel"（广泛的旅行）是正确答案。

items.title: Why is this story being told?
items.analysis: 这个故事被讲述是为了宣传Charity-Water，因为演讲者明确表示她要讲述这个故事是为了让听众了解Charity-Water所做的工作。整个演讲都围绕着Charity-Water的项目及其对一个村庄和Helen生活的影响展开，这明显是在宣传该组织的工作成效和重要性。

items.title: Why do the charity workers usually surprise communities?

options.title: B
options.content: It is difficult to spread news.

items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为原文明确指出慈善机构的工作人员选择出其不意地到达社区是为了更容易地监测供水点的运作情况。这种做法使他们的工作更加高效，避免了大量人群聚集而可能影响监测工作的情况。因此，选择突然造访社区的目的是为了使他们的工作更加便利。

items.title: When villagers heard of the charity workers' arrival, they
items.analysis: 文中提到"rumours of your presence spread like wildfire"（你们到来的消息像野火一样传开），表明村民们已经知道慈善工作者要来。随后直接描述"We jump out of the truck and walk into a party"，说明村民们为慈善工作者的到来准备了欢迎聚会。这与选项A "had a party"完全吻合，因此A是正确答案。

items.title: Helen is feeling
items.analysis: Helen明确表达了她对新生活的喜悦和满足。她使用了"happy"这个词，并且"beamed"（容光焕发）表明她非常兴奋。她列举了许多生活改善的方面，如有时间吃饭、孩子可以上学、可以在花园工作等。这些变化都是由于新水井的建立，表明她对新生活感到非常兴奋和满意，符合"ecstatic about her new life"的描述。

items.title: What did the speaker notice about Helen?

options.title: B
options.content: the care she took with her appearance

options.title: C
options.content: that she was wearing a green uniform

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为说话者注意到了Helen精心打扮的多个方面，包括她容光焕发的脸庞、头发上的鲜花和特殊场合穿的漂亮绿色连衣裙。这些细节都表明Helen很在意自己的外表，并为此付出了努力，正好符合选项B "the care she took with her appearance"（她对自己外表的用心）。

items.title: Making someone feel beautiful was

options.title: A
options.content: part of the speaker's job description.

options.title: B
options.content: an unexpected bonus for the speaker.

options.title: C
options.content: of little importance to the speaker.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B选项，因为文中明确指出让人感到美丽并不在说话人的工作描述中。说话人提到她的工作主要关注可持续发展、健康和卫生等实际问题，但让Helen感到美丽是一个意外的结果。这种效果超出了她的工作范围，因此可以被视为一个意外的收获或额外的好处。

groups.desc:
Questions 18-20
Choose THREE letters A - G.
groups.title: In what THREE ways did the new well improve Helen's life?

collect_options.title: In what THREE ways did the new well improve Helen's life?

list.title: C
list.content: It gave her more free time

list.title: E
list.content: She had more choices and options

list.title: F
list.content: She made new friends in her village

list.title: G
list.content: It allowed her to go to school.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C、D和E，因为文中明确提到了新水井如何改善了Helen的生活。新水井给了Helen更多的自由时间（C），让她获得了领导职位（D），并且给了她更多的选择和机会（E）。这些变化直接反映了新水井对Helen生活的积极影响，符合题目要求的"改善生活的三种方式"。
这道题的正确答案是C、D和E，因为文中明确提到了新水井如何改善了Helen的生活。新水井给了Helen更多的自由时间（C），让她获得了领导职位（D），并且给了她更多的选择和机会（E）。这些变化直接反映了新水井对Helen生活的积极影响，符合题目要求的"改善生活的三种方式"。
这道题的正确答案是C、D和E，因为文中明确提到了新水井如何改善了Helen的生活。新水井给了Helen更多的自由时间（C），让她获得了领导职位（D），并且给了她更多的选择和机会（E）。这些变化直接反映了新水井对Helen生活的积极影响，符合题目要求的"改善生活的三种方式"。
